stories.title: Burnham to spend first day working from No 10 North
stories.summary: PM Andy Burnham announced he will base himself and senior ministers at a new northern hub of No. 10 in Manchester, with Greater Manchester Combined Authority staff recruited to the operation.

stories.title: Regional mayors to take key roles in UK policy decisions as part of No 10 North plans
stories.summary: Burnham said 'no one in government loses when power is shared,' with regional mayors set to participate directly in national policy-making.

stories.title: Burnham plans to collaborate with social media creators
stories.summary: Andy Burnham is pursuing an active social media strategy as PM, planning to work with content creators — a continuation of the approach he honed as Greater Manchester Mayor.

topics.name: Burnham Gov Intelligence
topics.description: Procurement, tech/AI, spending, vendor & priority signals

stories.title: 🚦 PRIORITY/PAUSE: Early prisoner release scheme paused by Burnham for urgent review
stories.summary: Andy Burnham paused the early release scheme set for September and ordered an urgent review. No prisoners will be released until review is complete.

stories.title: 💰 SPENDING: Business rates slashed 20% for pubs, clubs and music venues
stories.summary: A 20% business rates cut for hospitality and live music venues was announced from April. Burnham called it a 'first step,' reversing the previous administration's planned hike.

stories.title: 🤖 TECH: Technology department closed, replaced with single AI Cabinet minister
stories.summary: Burnham abolished the Technology Department, placing AI responsibility with a single Cabinet minister — criticised by Kemi Badenoch as a downgrade of digital government ambitions.

topics.name: Parliament
topics.description: House of Commons, House of Lords, PMQs, debates, legislation

stories.title: House of Commons in session — 23 July 2026
stories.summary: Parliament was in session on 23 July, with Hansard recording debates as Burnham faces his first weeks at the despatch box as Prime Minister.

stories.title: House of Lords debates — Young People and Gambling Harm (23 July 2026)
stories.summary: The Lords held a dedicated debate on gambling harm for children and young people, pressing the government on plans to strengthen protections for minors.

topics.name: Labour Party
topics.description: Labour party news, policy, internal politics

stories.title: Burnham shakes up Whitehall with new government departmental structure
stories.summary: Andy Burnham announced a major restructure of Whitehall departments, including abolishing the Technology Department and creating new cross-departmental mission structures.

stories.title: What will Keir Starmer do next?
stories.summary: With Burnham now PM, speculation grows about what former Labour PM Keir Starmer will do next in politics, law or international affairs.

stories.title: Badenoch criticises closing of technology department — "one guy in Cabinet" for AI
stories.summary: Kemi Badenoch criticised Burnham's decision to close the Technology Department, calling it a downgrade of digital government ambitions and warning of reduced Whitehall capacity.

stories.title: Badenoch demands Burnham rule out tax rises
stories.summary: After the business rates cut, Badenoch called on Burnham to rule out tax rises more broadly, framing herself as guardian of business against Labour fiscal plans.

stories.title: Dame Maureen Lipman backs Badenoch for first time
stories.summary: Long-time Labour supporter Dame Maureen Lipman said she is backing Kemi Badenoch, calling her a 'truthful politician' who 'held the House during the Starmer years.'

topics.name: Liberal Democrats
topics.description: Lib Dem party news, Ed Davey, policy positions

topics.name: Reform UK
topics.description: Nigel Farage, Reform UK party news and announcements

stories.title: Inquiry launched into Farage poster row at anti-racism music event
stories.summary: An investigation was launched after claims Reform UK councillors tried to shut down a music event that displayed anti-racism posters featuring Nigel Farage.

stories.title: Met Police investigating Reform UK donations linked to convicted fraudster George Cottrell
stories.summary: The Metropolitan Police are investigating donations to Reform UK by Fiona Cottrell, mother of fraudster 'Posh George' who has been funding Farage. Reform told councillors to call it a 'smear.'

stories.title: Count Binface: Farage 'self-detonated' in Clacton by-election
stories.summary: Post-election commentary suggests Farage encountered significant difficulties in his own Clacton constituency seat during a by-election.

stories.title: Burnham tells Swinney indyref2 is 'off limits'
stories.summary: On his first Scotland visit as PM, Burnham told First Minister John Swinney a second independence referendum is off the table, while agreeing to 'engage' on further devolution.

stories.title: Burnham's Scotland visit hands Swinney a political opportunity
stories.summary: Burnham's decision not to speak to media during his Scotland visit gave the SNP First Minister room to press devolution demands and highlight tensions with the new government.

stories.title: Scottish Labour needs 'big changes' after Anas Sarwar exit
stories.summary: Michael Marra, widely tipped to succeed Sarwar as Scottish Labour leader, said the party needs significant changes to rebuild its position north of the border.

topics.name: Cabinet Office
topics.description: Government reshuffles, senior appointments, cross-department policy

stories.title: Burnham announces major Whitehall departmental restructure
stories.summary: As part of his No 10 North agenda, Burnham restructured Whitehall departments, abolishing the Technology Department and aligning government machinery with his mission-led approach.

stories.title: UK diplomatic staff withdrawn from Tehran
stories.summary: UK diplomatic staff, including the ambassador, have been withdrawn from Tehran amid rising regional tensions, signalling a significant deterioration in UK-Iran relations.

topics.name: Elections & By-elections
topics.description: Upcoming or recent election results, polls

stories.title: Clacton by-election: Farage reported to face major difficulties
stories.summary: Post-election commentary suggests Nigel Farage encountered significant difficulties in the Clacton by-election, with Count Binface saying Farage 'self-detonated' in his own seat.

stories.title: Greater Manchester mayoral election debate held
stories.summary: Candidates debated skills and growth for the city region, as the race heats up to succeed Burnham who has moved to No 10.

stories.title: House of Lords Chamber in session — 23 July 2026
stories.summary: The Lords held their regular chamber session on 23 July, with debates across a range of policy areas as Parliament heads towards summer recess.

stories.title: Lords debate: Young People and Gambling Harm
stories.summary: Peers debated government plans to reduce gambling harm among children and young people, with calls for stronger regulatory protections for minors.
